notes: First-party control matrix for the platform itself (we sell ATO evidence packs; this is ours). Status is honest: 'implemented' controls carry evidence_files that exist in this repo; 'partial' have a code/doc basis but an unmet external gate; 'planned' and 'not_applicable' carry no implementation claim. generate_oscal.py enforces the no-implemented-without-evidence rule and renders OSCAL.

controls.title: Account Management
controls.narrative: Users are provisioned/deprovisioned via SCIM from the customer IdP; lifecycle automation is in place. Periodic access recertification is not yet automated.

controls.title: Access Enforcement
controls.narrative: Role-based access is enforced on every route via require_role/require_scope dependencies resolved from a signed identity context.

controls.title: Information Flow Enforcement
controls.narrative: Cross-tenant data flow is prevented at two independent layers: a SQLAlchemy ORM guard injects org scoping into every query (fail-closed with no org context), and Postgres Row-Level Security enforces it at the database with FORCE against a NOBYPASSRLS app role.

controls.title: Event Logging
controls.narrative: A taxonomy of security-relevant events (auth, access, policy, key, tenant-guard bypass, integrity) is emitted to a tamper-evident audit log.

controls.title: Content of Audit Records
controls.narrative: Each record carries timestamp, event type, subject, outcome, source, resource, tenant, and a correlation id (AU-3 fields).

controls.title: Protection of Audit Information
controls.narrative: Records are hash-chained (HMAC-SHA256 when a key is configured) so tampering breaks the chain; integrity verification walks the log.

controls.title: Audit Record Generation
controls.narrative: Audit generation is centralized in log_event and invoked at every security-bearing operation across the app.

controls.title: Control Assessments
controls.narrative: Third-party penetration test and independent assessment are scheduled; not yet performed.

controls.title: Configuration Settings
controls.narrative: Runtime images are hardened multi-stage non-root builds (uid 10001, no compiler/dev deps in the runtime layer); the production topology runs read-only rootfs, cap_drop ALL, no-new-privileges, with zero default credentials.

controls.title: System Backup
controls.narrative: Backup/restore and HA-DR procedures are documented; a full game-day exercise has not been run.

controls.title: Identification and Authentication (Organizational Users)
controls.narrative: Users authenticate via OIDC/SAML federation to short-lived RS256 JWTs with kid-based key rotation; API principals use bcrypt-hashed keys.

controls.title: Device Identification and Authentication
controls.narrative: The inline runtime agent authenticates to the control plane with a short-lived client certificate (mutual TLS), not a static credential.

controls.title: Authenticator Management
controls.narrative: Signing keys rotate by kid with overlap; agent certs rotate via cert-manager with hot reload; secrets resolve from env/Vault/ASM references, never literals.

controls.title: Incident Handling
controls.narrative: Incident response runbook and on-call rotation are not yet formalized for the platform's own operations.

controls.title: Vulnerability Monitoring and Scanning
controls.narrative: CI gates on secret scanning (gitleaks), SAST (bandit, gosec, CodeQL), dependency audit (pip-audit, npm audit), and Trivy image scanning (HIGH/CRITICAL).

controls.title: Developer Testing and Evaluation
controls.narrative: Every change lands via reviewed PR behind blocking unit/integration tests, Go race tests, and the security gates above.

controls.title: Development Process, Standards, and Tools
controls.narrative: A documented secure SDLC governs the workflow; CODEOWNERS requires review on security-sensitive paths; branch protection is codified.

controls.title: Transmission Confidentiality and Integrity
controls.narrative: Agent-to-control-plane traffic uses TLS 1.3 with server identity pinned to the platform CA (not the system trust store).

controls.title: Cryptographic Key Establishment and Management
controls.narrative: JWT signing keys and agent client certificates are short-lived and rotated (kid overlap; cert-manager renewal with hot reload).

controls.title: Cryptographic Protection
controls.narrative: RS256 for tokens, TLS 1.3 for agent transport, and authenticated field-level encryption for sensitive data at rest.

controls.title: Protection of Information at Rest
controls.narrative: Sensitive fields are encrypted at rest with a versioned keyring supporting rotation; keys resolve from a secret manager.

controls.title: Information Input Validation
controls.narrative: Inbound content is decoded/normalized (NFKC, zero-width/bidi strip, multi-encoding decode) before the detector suite evaluates it, defeating encoding-bypass.

controls.title: Supply Chain Controls and Processes
controls.narrative: Each release builds digest-addressed images, generates CycloneDX SBOMs, and gates on Trivy — via a maintained-once org-level reusable workflow.

controls.title: Provenance
controls.narrative: SLSA v1 build provenance is generated for every published image and is verifiable with slsa-verifier.

controls.title: Component Authenticity
controls.narrative: Images are cosign-signed (keyless/Fulcio, OIDC workflow identity) and SBOM-attested; signatures are verifiable against the workflow identity.

controls.title: Physical Access Control
controls.narrative: The platform runs on managed cloud / customer infrastructure; physical controls are inherited from the hosting provider.
